context: Tests whether the agent correctly manages Swift Concurrency Task lifecycle inside a UIViewController: storing Task references, cancelling in viewDidDisappear, checking Task.isCancelled before UI updates, using Task {} (not Task.detached), avoiding redundant @MainActor annotations on UIViewController subclass methods, and gating any iOS 26+ APIs with #available.

checklist.name: Task stored as property
checklist.description: A Task (or Task<Void, Never>) is stored as an instance property of FeedViewController, not as a local variable

checklist.name: Cancel in viewDidDisappear
checklist.description: The stored Task is cancelled inside viewDidDisappear(_:), not inside deinit

checklist.name: isCancelled check before UI update
checklist.description: Task.isCancelled (or task.isCancelled) is checked after the await on fetchArticles() and before any UI update (e.g. before applying a snapshot or reloading data)

checklist.name: Task {} not Task.detached
checklist.description: Uses Task { } to start the async work — does NOT use Task.detached { }

checklist.name: No redundant @MainActor on VC methods
checklist.description: No method inside the UIViewController subclass is annotated with @MainActor (the class inherits main-actor isolation implicitly)

checklist.name: [weak self] in escaping closure
checklist.description: The Task closure (or any other escaping closure) captures self as [weak self]

checklist.name: #available guard for iOS 26+ APIs
checklist.description: If any iOS 26+ Swift Concurrency API is used (e.g. defaultIsolation, @concurrent), it is wrapped in an #available(iOS 26, *) check with a fallback code path; OR no iOS 26+ APIs are used at all (criterion is waived if the implementation contains no iOS 26+ APIs)

checklist.name: super called in lifecycle overrides
checklist.description: Every overridden UIViewController lifecycle method (viewDidLoad, viewDidAppear, viewDidDisappear, etc.) calls the corresponding super method
